what_it_does: Records an image attachment entry for the design-assets channel, linking to a specific image resource by URI and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter metadata block with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
File body is empty after the frontmatter
standouts: Target URI contains literal spaces and parentheses: 'image://design-assets/icons/image (51)'
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter in a Markdown file for structured ledger metadata
Fields follow a fixed schema: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
Represents a channel attachment as a standalone file named by attachment id
concerns: Target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing/encoding issues
No alt text, title, dimensions, or MIME type for the image
No indication of who added the attachment or why
Minimal metadata makes the asset hard to identify without dereferencing the URI
notes: A bare ledger entry that registers an image attachment in the design-assets channel, pointing to an icon resource with an unusually formatted URI.
questions: What does 'image (51)' refer to, and is the URI stable?
Is there an actual file or message associated with this attachment?
What is the intended use or display context of this icon image?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an attachment, and the file does exactly that with typed metadata and a target URI.
intention_signals: To track and make discoverable a specific icon image asset attached to the design-assets channel.
novelty: The target URI embeds literal spaces and parentheses, which is an uncommon and potentially fragile URI format.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel and an image asset, serving as an attachment record that a fused system can retain for asset tracking and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: Markdown ledger file att-1d79106d records an image attachment of type images in the design-assets channel pointing to the URI image://design-assets/icons/image (51), added at 2026-06-22T12:26:26.414162+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to catalog a specific icon image attachment within the design-assets channel.
